summary: The NESTROFT test screens for β-thalassemia by assessing fetal hemoglobin (HbF) levels, as decreased HbF indicates impaired γ-globin chain production characteristic of this condition. This makes it distinct from tests related to other types of anemias such as hereditary spherocytosis or autoimmunity.
